<!-- topic: agent-rules -->
<!-- sources: ADR-004, ADR-010, ADR-018, ADR-023, ADR-025 -->
<!-- compiled_by: gov-compile v0.6.0-rc4 -->
<!-- compiled_at: 2026-05-04T09:46:01Z -->

# Agent-Rules

[edikt:directives:start]: #
- Advisor agents MUST NEVER write files or modify state. (ref: ADR-004)
- Advisor agents MUST be invoked automatically by edikt based on domain signals. (ref: ADR-004)
- Advisor agents MUST run in isolated forked subagents (context: fork). (ref: ADR-004)
- Advisor findings MUST use the three-level severity model: Critical (must be addressed before shipping), Warning (should be addressed, not blocking), OK (domain looks healthy). (ref: ADR-004)
- Proactive advisory MUST fire at three moments: session start, pre-flight (after plan creation), and post-implementation (via /edikt:review). (ref: ADR-004)
- The distinction between advisors (in scope) and executors (out of scope) MUST be maintained precisely. (ref: ADR-004)
- A phase with BLOCKED verdicts is NOT considered verified and MUST NOT be marked complete. (ref: ADR-010)
- Both `templates/agents/evaluator.md` (subagent) and `templates/agents/evaluator-headless.md` (headless) MUST declare BLOCKED as a valid per-criterion verdict and a valid overall verdict. (ref: ADR-010)
- Default mode MUST be headless. `.edikt/config.yaml` ships with `evaluator.mode: headless` as the default and recommended value. (ref: ADR-010)
- Every evaluator failure mode MUST produce user-visible output with the failure reason, the criterion affected (where applicable), and the one-line command the user can run to recover. (ref: ADR-010)
- Failures in evaluator path probing MUST surface with actionable remediation. (ref: ADR-010)
- If Bash is denied, the subagent evaluator MUST return BLOCKED for that criterion with an explicit recovery hint. (ref: ADR-010)
- If an acceptance criterion requires Bash execution and Bash is denied, the verdict for that criterion MAY be BLOCKED. (ref: ADR-010)
- On headless failure (spawn error, non-zero exit, auth error, timeout), `commands/sdlc/plan.md` MUST fall back to subagent mode and emit a visible warning banner naming the failure reason and the remediation. (ref: ADR-010)
- Read-only inspection MUST NOT substitute for runtime verification. (ref: ADR-010)
- The evaluator MAY fall back to subagent mode only when headless fails, and MUST emit a visible warning banner when it does. (ref: ADR-010)
- The evaluator MUST NOT return PASS when required verification steps could not execute. (ref: ADR-010)
- The evaluator MUST return a BLOCKED verdict — never PASS — when it cannot execute a verification step required by an acceptance criterion. (ref: ADR-010)
- The evaluator MUST run headless by default. (ref: ADR-010)
- The progress table MUST gain a `blocked` state in addition to the existing `pass`/`fail`/`evaluating` states. (ref: ADR-010)
- The subagent evaluator template MUST self-check before claiming verdicts: before evaluating any criterion that requires Bash, it MUST probe whether Bash is available. (ref: ADR-010)
- `/edikt:doctor` MUST probe the evaluator path: verify `claude` is on PATH, headless probe succeeds, configured evaluator template exists, and `evaluator.mode` is set explicitly (not inferred default). (ref: ADR-010)
- `commands/sdlc/plan.md` MUST attempt headless first when `evaluator.mode: headless`. (ref: ADR-010)
- `commands/sdlc/plan.md` MUST parse and surface BLOCKED verdicts in the phase evaluation output. (ref: ADR-010)
- For every criterion whose `id` references a shell command in the plan's criteria sidecar (patterns: `pytest`, `bash`, `make`, `npm test`, `./test/`, `uv run`), the verdict's corresponding criterion MUST have `evidence_type: "test_run"`. (ref: ADR-018)
- Grandfathered verdicts MUST bypass the evidence gate on a one-time basis per phase. (ref: ADR-018)
- If any required-test criterion has a different evidence_type, the overall verdict MUST be forced to BLOCKED regardless of the evaluator's stated verdict, and the reason MUST be logged with the unmet criterion IDs. (ref: ADR-018)
- New evaluations (future phase completions) MUST use the strict schema. (ref: ADR-018)
- Old verdicts (identified by their `$schema` URL) MUST NEVER be silently reinterpreted under a new schema version. (ref: ADR-018)
- On upgrade from edikt < 0.5.0, for every phase currently marked `done`, the upgrade MUST write a stub verdict with `meta.grandfathered: true` and `meta.migrated_from: "<prior-version>"`. (ref: ADR-018)
- Structured verdicts MUST persist as runtime state sidecars at `docs/product/plans/verdicts/<plan-slug>/<phase-id>.json`. (ref: ADR-018)
- The evaluator MUST emit a single JSON object conforming to `templates/agents/evaluator-verdict.schema.json`. (ref: ADR-018)
- `templates/agents/evaluator-verdict.schema.json` MUST carry a top-level `$id` field of the form `https://edikt.dev/schemas/evaluator-verdict/v<N>.json`. (ref: ADR-018)
- All evaluator agent templates (`templates/agents/evaluator-*.md`) MUST produce a JSON object conforming to the structured evaluator-output shape. (ref: ADR-023)
- Content-based keyword detection over free text MUST NOT be used as the primary domain resolution path — it is the deprecated legacy path as of v0.6.0. (ref: ADR-023)
- Evaluator agents MUST NOT emit prose verdicts. (ref: ADR-023)
- If the hook input does not contain the structured `evaluator_output` shape, the hook MAY log `{"event": "legacy_payload", "hook": "subagent-stop"}` to `events.jsonl`. (ref: ADR-023)
- If the hook input does not contain the structured `evaluator_output` shape, the hook MAY warn to stderr: `warn: legacy evaluator payload; falling back to keyword detection`. (ref: ADR-023)
- Severity ordering MUST be `critical(3) > warning(2) > info(1)`. (ref: ADR-023)
- The `evaluator_output.agent` field MUST be set to the agent's domain identifier (e.g., `"security"`, `"dba"`, `"sre"`, `"architect"`, `"performance"`, `"api"`). (ref: ADR-023)
- The gate MUST fire when `finding.severity_level >= threshold_level`. (ref: ADR-023)
- The gate-fired message MUST be assembled inside the hook process using `python3 -c 'import json; print(json.dumps(...))'` with untrusted values as argv (INV-003, INV-004). (ref: ADR-023)
- When `evaluator_output.agent` is absent (legacy unstructured payload), the hook MUST log a warning to `events.jsonl` and fall back to `gates.default`. (ref: ADR-023)
- When the gate fires, `subagent-stop.sh` MUST emit the structured BLOCKED message with agent name, severity, and threshold values. (ref: ADR-023)
- `subagent-stop.sh` MUST read `evaluator_output.agent` from the structured payload to determine the gate severity threshold. (ref: ADR-023)
- `subagent-stop.sh` MUST read the SubagentStop hook input JSON from stdin and extract verdict data from the top-level `evaluator_output` field. (ref: ADR-023)
- `subagent-stop.sh` MUST resolve the effective severity threshold in priority order: EDIKT_GATE_SEVERITY_THRESHOLD env var, then gates.<agent>, then gates.default, then compiled-in fallback `critical`. (ref: ADR-023)
- `templates/agents/evaluator-verdict.schema.json` MUST be the source of truth for the evaluator JSON structure; ADR-023 adds the `evaluator_output.agent` field requirement on top of it. (ref: ADR-023)
- Additive changes to the evaluator verdict schema (new optional fields, new enum values) MUST NOT bump the schema version `<N>`. (ref: ADR-025)
- Breaking changes to the evaluator verdict schema (removed fields, changed enum semantics, required-field additions) MUST bump `<N>` and ship with a migration routine in `bin/edikt upgrade`. (ref: ADR-025)
- For every criterion whose `id` references a shell command in the plan's criteria sidecar (patterns: `pytest`, `bash`, `make`, `npm test`, `./test/`, `uv run`), the verdict's corresponding criterion MUST have `evidence_type: "test_run"`. (ref: ADR-025)
- Grandfathered verdicts MUST bypass the evidence gate on a one-time basis per phase. (ref: ADR-025)
- If any required-test criterion has a different `evidence_type`, the overall verdict MUST be forced to BLOCKED regardless of the evaluator's stated verdict, and the reason MUST be logged with the unmet criterion IDs. (ref: ADR-025)
- NEVER hardcode a literal verdicts path. (ref: ADR-025)
- New evaluations (future phase completions) MUST use the strict schema. (ref: ADR-025)
- Old verdicts (identified by their `$schema` URL) MUST NEVER be silently reinterpreted under a new schema version. (ref: ADR-025)
- Structured verdicts MUST persist as runtime state sidecars at `<paths.plans>/verdicts/<plan-slug>/<phase-id>.json`, where `<paths.plans>` is the value of `paths.plans` in `.edikt/config.yaml`. (ref: ADR-025)
- The evaluator MUST emit a single JSON object conforming to `templates/agents/evaluator-verdict.schema.json`. (ref: ADR-025)
- Tooling that reads or writes verdicts MUST resolve the path through the loaded `paths.plans` config value. (ref: ADR-025)
- `bin/edikt upgrade` MUST move any legacy `docs/product/plans/verdicts/` content to the resolved `<paths.plans>/verdicts/` location before running grandfathering for projects whose `paths.plans` no longer matches that path, preserving filenames and contents byte-for-byte. (ref: ADR-025)
- `templates/agents/evaluator-verdict.schema.json` MUST carry a top-level `$id` of the form `https://edikt.dev/schemas/evaluator-verdict/v<N>.json`. (ref: ADR-025)
[edikt:directives:end]: #
[edikt:directives:sha256]: # eefe0a4c49f8eda25f31edd693b96a5e9a80f57df93fdc051c6caa915d2c251c

[edikt:prohibitions:start]: #
## Prohibitions
- MUST NOT add erratum notes to an accepted ADR — erratum-style metadata is not a recognized concept in edikt's ADR model and such a note still mutates the accepted ADR in violation of INV-002. (ref: ADR-025)
- MUST NOT allow the evaluator to be invoked directly (bypassing the orchestrator config) without enforcing the BLOCKED-verdict contract. (ref: ADR-010)
- MUST NOT collapse to headless-only (remove subagent mode entirely) — strands users in environments where headless cannot run. (ref: ADR-010)
- MUST NOT edit an accepted ADR in place to fix a directive error — this violates INV-002 (accepted ADRs are immutable) and breaks the audit trail. Supersede with a new ADR instead. (ref: ADR-025)
- MUST NOT fire advisor agents from PostToolUse hooks via nested claude -p calls — PostToolUse hooks cannot run Claude agents and nested calls break session context. (ref: ADR-004)
- MUST NOT keep both evaluator modes with only a user-facing config switch and no automatic fallback or BLOCKED verdict — this preserves silent-PASS failures. (ref: ADR-010)
- MUST NOT route tasks to agents that write code or modify files (full execution orchestration) — execution orchestration is out of scope per ADR-004. (ref: ADR-004)
- MUST NOT use a dual-evaluator approach as the primary fix for false PASS verdicts — doubles eval cost and does not prevent both evaluators from rationalizing the same PASS. (ref: ADR-018)
- MUST NOT use a hard cutover that discards all existing PASS verdicts on upgrade — causes every live plan to regress and forces users mid-release into re-evaluation. (ref: ADR-018)
- MUST NOT use prose verdicts with a lint pass for machine-checkability — fragile; produces false positives and false negatives on keyword matching; does not solve the fundamental lack of machine-checkable evidence. (ref: ADR-018)
[edikt:prohibitions:end]: #
[edikt:prohibitions:sha256]: # 7c23fb67be44def4e359ea4269104639ad4f78b46b5903316ee2f83d40af3a58
